figures.caption: Figure 5. f(x)− f∗ vs. Step, Algorithm 1, Logistic Regression, a5a dataset, B = 16 across various β, lr, compression factor K% and q.

Table 1. Summary of the results to reach the ϵ-precision for the problem (1), i.e., mink=0,...,K−1 ∑p i=1 tiE [ ∥∇if(X k)∥(i)⋆ ] ≤ ϵ. Dependences on the numerical constants,“quality” of the starting point, smoothness constants, and Hessian variance constants, etc., are omitted in the complexity bounds. We assume the compression ratios of unbiased compressor and contraction compressor are Θ(ω + 1) and Θ( 1 δ ), respectively. Abbreviations: “Communication cost” = the total communication cost needed to reach the ϵ-precision in expectation; “Oracle complexity” = the total number of (stochastic) first-order oracle calls needed to reach the ϵ-precision in expectation. The value of parameter c in the Communication cost is the same as in the corresponding Oracle complexity.
